summary: Completed testing of NEW features in Local Recruitment ATS: Duplicate candidate detection and Compare candidates functionality. Backend APIs working correctly with minor validation issue. Frontend UI components present and functional for new features.

flaky_endpoints.issue: AI sometimes returns experience as integer instead of string, causing Pydantic validation error
flaky_endpoints.symptoms: 500 error: 'Input should be a valid string [type=string_type, input_value=8, input_type=int]'
flaky_endpoints.workaround: Retry resume processing - inconsistent AI response format

passed_tests: NEW: Duplicate Detection API - GET /api/check-duplicate (email and mobile detection working)
NEW: Get Duplicates API - GET /api/duplicates/{job_id} (returns duplicate candidates correctly)
NEW: Compare Candidates API - POST /api/compare-candidates (side-by-side comparison with skill analysis working)
NEW: Resume processing with duplicate detection - returns duplicates_found array with match types
NEW: Duplicate candidate normalization - email and phone number matching algorithms working
NEW: Candidates page shows compare checkboxes when candidates exist
NEW: Compare button UI elements present and functional in candidates table
NEW: Filter dropdowns (job and category) working correctly
NEW: Search functionality accessible and responsive
NEW: Export Excel button present and functional
NEW: Navigation between pages maintains UI consistency
NEW: Processing page file upload interface working
NEW: Job creation and management working for test setup
action_item_for_main_agent: Fix AI resume parsing to consistently return experience as string format (e.g., '5 years') instead of integer (5) to prevent Pydantic validation errors in Candidate model

success_percentage.backend: 95% (23/24 tests passed - 1 flaky due to AI parsing inconsistency)
success_percentage.frontend: 100% (all new UI components present and functional)
success_percentage.new_features: 95% (duplicate detection fully working, compare candidates working with minor backend issue)
